content: AUD treatment follows stepped-care approach: (1) Screening and brief intervention (SBIRT)—AUDIT-C screening, motivational interviewing for risky drinkers; (2) Outpatient treatment—mutual help groups (AA, SMART Recovery), counseling (CBT, MET, 12-step facilitation); (3) Pharmacotherapy—naltrexone (reduces craving/reward), acamprosate (maintains abstinence), disulfiram (aversion therapy), off-label options (topiramate, gabapentin); (4) Medically managed withdrawal—for severe dependence with CIWA-Ar monitoring, benzodiazepines for seizure prevention, thiamine supplementation; (5) Residential/inpatient treatment—for severe AUD or failed outpatient attempts; (6) Recovery support—peer recovery coaches, sober living environments, continuing care. Population-level interventions: taxation, minimum unit pricing, availability restrictions, marketing regulations, drunk driving laws. Harm reduction strategies include moderate drinking goals for some patients.
